review_text: The Introduction to AI class was an excellent learning experience. The professor explained complex AI concepts in a very clear and understandable way, and questions were always encouraged. I especially appreciated how supportive and approachable the professor was throughout the semester.

It is unbelievable how rude this professor is, she doesn't respect her student opinion nor time. I just don't see how an intro elective class is harder than my main major classes (biochem). Honestly the work load for this class is astonishing, lengthy hws everyday, 2 quizzes every week. I barely have time for any other class, let alone free time.
Unreasonable work load for an intro class. Do not take her if you want to stay sane.

I would not recommend this professor, overloads an intro class with 2 quizzes + hws weekly, constantly belittles students in a condescending, dismissive way. Also said to lower grade for not attending outside of class hrs tutors session. Before I dropped, she gave 2 quizzes + a midterm in the same week....

I am taking organic chemistry right now and it is much easier compared to this INTRO class. There are labs/quizzes EVERY class. Labs are basically quizzes and quizzes are based on a ton of reading. It is just unreasonable for a professor to quiz students every single class on so much material. Take her if you're okay with 2 quizzes every week!

Honestly, he wasn't that bad. Like his lectures were pretty straightforward. His exams were the coding practices he gave in class, as long as you practiced them you should be good. He gives inspirational speeches now and then. You will learn coding really well!

😒😒I could have passed this class; I had a 69.5 in the class average. I remember he assigned the exam and a project on the same week. LIKE WHO DOES THAT. He talks so boring and sometimes he talks about things that don't even help us. Please save yourself the trouble.

This professor may be nice in person, but his exams were a literally a nightmare. It's mostly coding, he takes points off for no reason. I had a 69 average; I could have passed. But he didn't pass me. Also, he never gave any extra credit and gives unnecessary information in class that won't help us.

He's literally the worst professor ever. He takes what should be such an easy class 3304 and makes it into a nightmare. His exams are mostly coding and also takes points for no reason. Barely assigns easy homework or gives extra credit. Highly recommend people to stay away unless you know coding.

He's a great professor, most of your time will be spent on memorizing the exercises he gives for the exams, which is great as the topics will be hammered into you (always practice what he gives), but I recommend doing a project. He had one for us but I fumbled it badly, and even if I did well on the exams, I feel bad for not doing the project.

I took him for SWE, honestly easy class. Most of the material is conceptual, memorizing keywords and diagrams. There is some content I enjoyed, but the lectures had 0 engagement. I honestly cant HEAR him at all. Bro just zoomes by the powerpoint 😭 lol but he grades quite easily. The course project is fun NGL IF you have a good group. Good luck!

The one thing I want to let other students know is that if you take him he will open the quiz on the day of the exam. Including he is a last min grading person on the assignments but I also took him in the 5 week summer class.

The professor was soft-spoken as people said, just study the slides and you'll be good for the exam. He even gave us a review video on what could be expected which helped. The group project is an easy-intermediate difficulty if you have a contributing team. Overall, it was a 10/10 class, I would retake him if he offered other courses for my major.

He is soft spoken as what others have said, but he is a great professor and his tests aren't too difficult as long as you study from the slides. Just make sure you do really well on the group projects since that is 40% of the grade. I got a B since I got overwhelmed in my other classes and didn't study for this one enough. Still, highly recommend!!

Great professor. Soft spoken and reads off lecture slides. Had quizzes every week, 2 exams that kinda covered a lot of material but he shared exams review zoom meetings from the previous semester. Project assignments were a lot of work and although I don't think our project was that good, he gave us a good grade lol. Also a very slow grader.
Overall, good professor!

Professor Lin is a nice guy, but he seems to have lost interest in actually teaching. All of our classes consisted of was reading from PPT's. By the end of the course, only one other student and I attended class (15 person class). Lecture materials did not match exams - all exam questions were pulled from a test bank readily available on Quizlet.
I will advice you avoid this lecturer

This was the hardest class I've ever taken. The way the prof teaches and grades are awful. If you take his classes you will end up teaching yourself. He got a heavy slow accent that is hard to understand through zoom. after taking this class I wonder why UHD still has these kinds of profs. I would never recommend him to anyone. waste of time.
Possibly the worst class I've ever taken. He reads off slides, verbatim without ever opening up the software. He's a tough grader for being such an awful teacher.

Professor Kamto explains concepts clearly and breaks down difficult material step-by-step. His assignments match what he teaches in lecture, and he gives helpful feedback so you always know how to improve. He is direct and firm, but it keeps you accountable and helps you stay on track. If you show up and do the work, you will do well in his class.
Idk why he got such a low rating here but for me he is one of the best professor I've ever had, he even give you make up quizzes that you missed throughout the semester! And he is very easy and chill

I would not take this professor again. He reads off of slides and explains them.Doesnt actually teach how to code. He gets kind of mad when you ask questions like you are supposed to know the answer already.

I was afraid to take professor Kamto because of the bad reviews. But I just finished 2301 for summer and it went pretty smooth for me. Had two hw and two quizzes each week, 2 exams and a final. The final exam questions came from the quizzes and hw. Got C but it was because my own problem. I will take professor Kamto again for 2302.

Took for CS1411 Intro Python in Fall 2024, extremely rude, would belittle us and make us feel like our questions were stupid and obvious to answer, average exam score was around 70%, quizzes/homework often had incorrect answers, and if you didn't email him, you wouldn't get the points back. Keep in mind this is coming from a student who made an A.

Great professor. A lot of information covered but she really wants to help us understand the materials. Some coding assignments in C but really easy since she goes through the assignments in class with us. She makes the subject easier to understand and learn.

She is very honest, hardworking, caring, very good in grading but at the same time very disciplined and principled. She is one of the best Professors I ever had. My best wishes for her and her family.

Dr. Ling Xu is very good at teaching but she likes discipline and she is very principled. I did not see or noticed anything objectionable. I rate her as one of the best I ever experienced. She is a good but honest grader. My best wishes to her.
Do all work and you will pass. She is very informative and loves helping her students succeed. Allows you to work alone if you prefer. Highly recommend taking her if you can.

An honest, reasonable and hardworking professor and does not leave anything unexplained. Never asks anything out of context in Exams. Always reasonable and fair, never exercised her domain to harass or suppress students while grading. I would immediately take her class and I will comfortably rate her 100/100.

Arguably one of the worst professors in my career. He is passive aggressive in his responses, one who will give you backhanded statements for asking questions and belittle you for not knowing the course material. I highly recommend you, to avoid his course if possible.

He goes through the ppt and makes sure you understand. He provides old lecture recordings so you can re-watch. Exam and final on LDB but you get to use 1 cheat sheet. Make sure you & your  teammates stay on top of your project phase assignments. Don't procrastinate on those. 2nd exam was all just SQL statements. Class was tough but I learned a lot.

Class isn't engaging and he just goes off the powerpoint for the most part. The group project is also annoying to complete. There are guidelines on how to complete it, but if you make a mistake on one part, he won't let you know until the next part is almost due. The tests aren't impossible but you definitely need to study really well to get an A.

He behaves like a mentally sick person. If you ask a question, he will retaliate by angry remarks. Very ridiculous and self-contradictory, as he trashes your ER-Diagram to be wrong but never even attempts to correct it or explain why and where it was wrong and what was right. I attended all of his classes, but I learned all only through YouTube.

1st assignment requires you to know C language.  One of the student asked for help during class and his response was to search in google for help.  All quizzes are timed, ex: 2 minutes for 2 questions.  First few quizzes are easy and then they get very difficult.  He makes topics difficult to understand that what they truly are.  Worst CS professor

As long as you pay attention in class, study the hw problems, and do the course work you should be fine. His course will be EZPZ if you do these things. He is a little unorganized so make sure to ask questions if you're unsure about the course schedule and due dates. He has a harsh accent in the beginning, but you'll become accustomed to it.

EZPZ lemon squeezy. Kind of hard to understand through his accent but eventually you'll understand what he's trying to say. He talks very fast since there's so much material to go over. He gives out exam reviews and they're exactly the same as the exams so start studying early and you will pass easily. Very interesting class because of his teaching

He starts off difficult to understand but stay with it, you get use to it. Watch his videos and you will be great! He takes a very complicated subject and makes it digestible. Watch the videos with caption on and don't fall behind.

One of the best CS professors at UHD. I've taken CS 1411 and CS 3306 with him, and his passion is clear. He promotes class engagement and encourages stronger students to help others. Expectations for exams are clear, and he is always willing to help if you are stuck. He truly cares about student success and makes concepts easier through discussion.

Best Computer Science professor I've ever encountered. He is the kindest and most patient professor in and outside of class. He is more than willing to go over a problem as many times as you need in order to make sure you understand. He makes really hard concepts feel "Easy peasy lemon squeeze" and super interactive in class. 100% recommend!
Easy course but It didn't prepared me for real world scenarios.
good professor, and if you want a easy time of it take her class, seems to know what they're talking about in terms of projects.

Data structure course is suppose to prepare us for CS job interviews. But, the course materials and assignments were rather elementary. you may feel you are doing well but not actually learning the topics that matter in this course.

There are hands on projects using Arduino board during the class. It is a fun class and I have learned a lot. If you want to create projects for the real world as a computer science major, take her classes.
She is knowledgeable and inspiring, caring and supportive. Professor Zhang makes complex topics like data structures clear to understand. She provides a lot of examples in class.
